Require tasks to have a given status

Source: R/tasks.R
task_require.Rd

Aborts unless every task in id has one of the statuses in status ("completed" by default). This is a lightweight gate for coordinating a production script: call it before a step that depends on earlier ones being done. It only reads the task database and never touches your data, so it belongs beside the analysis rather than inside it.

Usage

task_require(db, id, status = "completed")

Arguments

db

Path to the task database, or an open DBI connection.

id

One or more task ids – each an integer id, a uuid, or a key.

status

Allowed status values; each task must be in one of them.

Value

id, invisibly.

See also

task_get(), task_done()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
task_require(db, 1)            # stop unless task 1 is done
task_require(db, c(1, 2))      # ... or several upstream steps
} # }
